name: Climb to La Covatilla (CE pass) — circular from Béjar
name_original: Subida a la Covatilla (puerto de CE) — circular desde Béjar
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Béjar are situated within the Sierra de Béjar, a region characterized by its diverse mountain landscapes and significant elevations. The area features coniferous and hardwood forests, with the River Cuerpo de Hombre flowing from the snowy peaks. Cyclists encounter challenging terrain, including ascents to high points like La Covatilla Ski Resort, offering varied experiences across its natural environment. Best road cycling routes around Béjar The most popular road cycling route is Climb to La Covatilla (CE pass) — circular from Béjar, a 45.3 miles (72.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 20 minutes to complete. This difficult route features a substantial ascent towards the La Covatilla Ski Resort. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Béjar – Baños de Montemayor loop from Béjar, a difficult 43.8 miles (70.4 km) path. This route offers varied mountain scenery and challenging climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Béjar – Honduras Pass loop from Béjar, a 73.6 miles (118.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 7 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Béjar is defined by the Sierra de Béjar mountains, the River Cuerpo de Hombre, and challenging ascents to high passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Béjar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. More than 1700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Béjar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Béjar?
A: There are over 70 road cycling routes around Béjar, catering to various skill levels. This includes 1 easy route, 26 moderate routes, and 44 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Béjar?
A: The Sierra de Béjar region offers diverse conditions. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant weather for road cycling, with milder temperatures. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter brings colder temperatures and snow to higher passes like La Covatilla, making those routes more challenging or inaccessible.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Béjar?
A: Yes, Béjar is known for its challenging terrain, with 44 difficult routes available. Many routes feature significant elevation gains, such as the ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023133545/climb-to-la-covatilla-ce-pass-circular-from-bejar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Covatilla Ski Resort&lt;/a&gt;, which climbs nearly 1,000 meters. Other demanding options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20140235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Béjar – Honduras Pass loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 118 km with substantial elevation.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Béjar?
A: While many routes are challenging, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. Additionally, the Béjar Greenway, built along an old railway line, offers a flatter, more accessible option suitable for less experienced cyclists or those looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: What scenic points or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Béjar?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can encounter stunning mountain scenery, coniferous and hardwood forests, and the clear waters of the River Cuerpo de Hombre. Notable landmarks include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/904737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt;, and the Roman city of Caparra's ruins along sections of the Vía de la Plata. The 16th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2671693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bosque Garden&lt;/a&gt; in Béjar also offers a tranquil stop.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, you can find natural attractions like waterfalls in the vicinity. For example, the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5701178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorrera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument that can be explored in the area.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Béjar?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Béjar are designed as loops, offering varied scenery without retracing your path. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023133545/climb-to-la-covatilla-ce-pass-circular-from-bejar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to La Covatilla (CE pass) — circular from Béjar&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31934091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Béjar – Baños de Montemayor loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the parking options for road cyclists in Béjar?
A: Béjar, as a town, offers various parking options, including public car parks, from which you can easily access the surrounding road cycling routes. Specific trailheads for routes often have designated parking areas, though it's always advisable to check local signage.

Q: Can I reach the road cycling routes in Béjar using public transport?
A: Béjar is accessible by public transport, including bus services that connect it to larger cities. While public transport can get you to Béjar, accessing specific route starting points might require a short ride or transfer, depending on the route's exact location.

Q: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation along the routes?
A: The town of Béjar itself, and many of the smaller villages you pass through on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31481884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Vallejera – Granite Theme Park loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt;, offer cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. These provide convenient stops for refreshments and overnight stays, especially on longer tours.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Béjar?
A: For families or those seeking less strenuous rides, the Béjar Greenway (Vía Verde) is an excellent option. Built on an old railway line, it offers a relatively flat and safe surface, making it suitable for cyclists of all ages and abilities.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Béjar?
A: The road cycling routes in Béjar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the challenging mountain passes, the breathtaking views of the Sierra de Béjar, and the diverse landscapes that make each ride unique and rewarding.
